---
title: "LifespanReflection"
author: "Brian C. Holt, MA"
date: "2026-05-24"
output:
  html_document:
    config:
      search: yes
      toc:
        after: |
          <!-- <li><a href="https://github.com/rstudio/bookdown" target="blank">Published with bookdown</a></li> -->
        before: |
          <li><a href="./">Lifespan Psychology Spring 2026 reflection</a></li>
        scroll_highlight: yes
      toolbar:
        position: fixed
    css: "style.css"
    number_sections: no
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 6
    toc_float: yes
description: This is a minimal example of using the rmarkdown package for Lifespan
  psychology
link-citations: yes
bibliography: Lifespan.bib
biblio-style: apalike
---

# Big idea

The purpose of this assignment is to review your work across the course, evaluate strengths and weaknesses, identify changes in your thinking, and demonstrate evidence of learning.

This assignment asks you to synthesize and evaluate your work across the quarter.

You will continue to use your google document. So, at the bottom of your google doc, enter a new heading/title for this assignment. 

# Important Rule: Portfolio Evidence Cutoff

Only assignments submitted on or before May 25th, 2026 may be used as portfolio evidence.

Assignments submitted after this date are still important, but they may not be used as evidence in this portfolio reflection.


# Part 1: Artifact ("assignment") Selection (20%)

From your one google document, select:

- **2 assignments you consider strongest**
- **1 assignment needing improvement**
- **1 assignment that changed your thinking**

For each provide:

- Assignment title
- Approximate submission date
- Basic and abbreviated reason for selection; if a strong example, what made it strong? If weak, why weak? 


# Part 2: Critical thinking Evaluation (20%)

Evaluate each of your 4 choices using some of the 9 the Critical thinking standards:

- Clear
- Accurate
- Precise
- Relevant
- Depth
- Breadth
- Logic
- Significance
- Fairness

[A rubric can be found here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1DRobbPDaLLLg-kSpeh95spS8tBoPvlK7lBFqvfrzHEk/edit?tab=t.0){target='_blank'}

For each of your 4 artifacts, I want you to provide the following for 2 critical thinking standards--but please mix them up; don't just do clarity over and over, for example:

- Standard
- Assignment used
- Quoted sentence from original work
- Why it demonstrates or fails to demonstrate this standard

# Part 3: Revision Exercise (20%)

Choose **one sentence or paragraph** from one of your "needs work" assignments.

Provide:

- Original text
- Revised text
- Explanation of change in the context of critical thinking


# Part 4: Growth Despite Incomplete Work (10%)

If you have missing or late assignments, respond to the following:

- Identify one assignment that was missing or late.
- What made that assignment difficult to complete on time?
- What learning, practice, or feedback did you miss by not completing it on time?
- What would you do differently in a future course?

If you do not have missing or late assignments, respond to this instead:

- What helped you stay current with the work?
- What habit or strategy would you recommend to another student?

# Part 5: Reflection and Growth (20%)

Respond to the following:

## Question 1

What idea from the course most changed your thinking?

## Question 2

What misconception did you have earlier in the quarter?

## Question 3

Which assignment best represents your learning?

## Question 4

What would you improve if you repeated the course?

# Part 6: AI Statement (10%)

AI use is permitted, but not to launder writing.  So, as explained in the syllabus, you need to be doing the writing.   Large copy/pastes is proof of cheating (with the exceptions that we already discussed). 

If AI tools were used, describe:

- Where used
- Purpose
- Whether helpful
- Limitations encountered

If AI tools were not used:

- State that no AI tools were used

# Points

| Category | Points |
|:---|---:|
| Part 1 | 20 |
| Part 2 | 20 |
| Part 3 | 20 |
| Part 4 | 10 |
| part 5 | 20 |
| part 6 | 10 |
| **Total** | **100** |
